buttons.name: sync-docs-weekly
buttons.label: SYNC DOCS
buttons.command: /sync-docs --append-system-prompt "Write the full drift report to /path/to/coderails-dashboard/routines/sync-docs/report-{date}.md before finishing."

buttons.name: inbox-brief
buttons.label: INBOX BRIEF
buttons.command: Run exactly this command via the Bash tool: bin/rachel "Read tasks/inbox-brief.md and follow it." < /dev/null . Its output will end with a line `BRIEF FILE: <path>`. Read that file and make your ENTIRE final message exactly its contents — nothing before it, nothing after it, no preamble, no code fence. If no `BRIEF FILE:` line appears (delivery failed or the sweep was skipped), instead report Rachel's stated outcome in a single line.

buttons.name: workflow-audit-weekly
buttons.label: WORKFLOW AUDIT (weekly)
buttons.command: Invoke coderails:workflow-audit in QUEUE-MODE over the last 7 days (--days 7). In-session skill creation is forbidden in this run — every propose verdict is written as a queue entry only. Finish by writing the run note to /path/to/coderails-dashboard/routines/workflow-audit/run-{date}.md (use exactly this path and filename) recording `proposals_written: <N>` and `proposals_attempted: <A>`, where N counts queue entries actually written this run. Emit the completion marker line `## [<YYYY-MM-DD>] workflow-audit complete` ONLY IF the run finished cleanly AND N == A. If any queue write failed (N != A) or the run did not complete, write the note WITHOUT the completion marker. N=0 with no failures is SUCCESS — emit the marker.
